alerts.title: Israel threatens third strike on Iran amid continued Gaza-Lebanon escalation
alerts.summary: Israeli Defense Minister Katz stated IDF is prepared for independent action against Iran and threatened a potential third strike. Simultaneous Israeli airstrikes in Gaza killed 6 including 2 children and an Al Jazeera cameraman, while IDF engaged in helicopter strikes in southern Syria following reported fire-upon incidents. Threat level remains 9/10 (CRITICAL). Sources: incidents (multiple outlets including Middle East Eye, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel), global_metrics (threat level).

alerts.title: Iran escalates energy infrastructure attacks on Gulf states post-Israeli strike
alerts.summary: Iran targeted Gulf countries' energy infrastructure following Israeli strike on Iranian gas field. Continued US-Iran reciprocal strikes in Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz with Iran attacking commercial shipping and US conducting counter-strikes on missile/drone sites. Brent crude at $71.92. Sources: incidents (ABC News, CENTCOM, NBC News), global_metrics (oil price trend).
alerts.region: Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz, Iran

alerts.title: US reverses nuclear precondition despite prior agreement, Iran rejects proposal
alerts.summary: US State Department conditioned unfreezing Iranian assets on nuclear file progress, contradicting explicit agreement that nuclear issues would not be discussed in current negotiation phase. Iran rejected the shift. Enrichment remains at 60% U-235 with 1-2 week breakout window estimated. Parallel talks underway in Doha. Sources: global_metrics (nuclear headlines, breakout timeline), telegram (ME Spectator), incidents (RFE/RL).

alerts.title: Ship runs aground in Strait of Hormuz amid escalating maritime disruption
alerts.summary: Vessel ran aground in strategically critical Strait of Hormuz, reported by Iranian state TV. Incident compounds ongoing cargo vessel attacks (US official confirmed Iran behind attack on cargo ship near Oman) and heightens risk of regional shipping disruption amid US-Iran strikes. Location: 26.65°N, 56.14°E. Sources: incidents (ABC10, CNBC, US official statements).

alerts.title: Iran-attributed group claims expanded cyber attacks targeting critical infrastructure
alerts.summary: Iran-linked cyber actors publicly escalated operations, claiming compromise of FBI unmanned aerial systems and issuing threats against World Cup infrastructure. Concurrent physical and cyber strike campaign demonstrates coordinated escalation strategy. Israeli cyber chief tracking related Iranian cyber threats. Sources: global_metrics (cyber headlines), incidents, telegram monitoring.

analysis_note: US-Iran direct military conflict has fractured ceasefire framework with reciprocal strikes accelerating; nuclear negotiations have collapsed over US precondition reversal; maritime chokepoints and Gulf energy infrastructure face active threat; regional proxy systems (Houthis, Lebanese actors, Iraqi militias) remain elevated; breakout risk window at 1-2 weeks.
